The thin-target stack consisted of metal foils of 8 mm
diameter. The Ba-glass samples analyzed in this study
done irradiated in this stack.  The target foils used
done made from Ba-glass with 40.21% barium content,
supplied by Schott Glaswerke, Mainz and had a Thickness
of 2 mm. The different target foils with diameters  of
15.9 mm done composed of stacks with thicknesses
between 9.35 g/cm**2 and 15.6 g/cm**2 in the order of
increasing atomic numbers of the target elements. For
each element, three foils done put together in order to
minimize recoil contamination and loss for the foil in
the middle. Between different target element catcher
foils of 0.038 mm Al(99.999) or 0.05 mm Au(99.9) were
inserted to avoid recoil and cross contamination and
Production of xenon isotopes in the artificial
Extraction procedure of rare gases from barium glass.
